hooks.text: Your wire was supposed to clear in two days and arrive in full. It showed up on day five, and one hundred fifty dollars short. Both true.
Your supplier is still chasing the wire on day five, and when it lands it is short. One thing between you and them explains both problems.
A wire to a Chinese supplier passes one to three banks you never see. Each one costs a full business day and skims fifteen to fifty dollars.
A business owner wired the deposit on Monday. Day five, the supplier is still asking where it is, and saying it landed one hundred fifty dollars light.
Every middle bank your wire passes through costs you a day and skims a fee. Three days late and one hundred fifty dollars gone, and nobody warned you.
